marketTone: Jul 20 Brent intraday topped $90 after Jul 18–19 CENTCOM strikes and Kuwait/Jordan infrastructure hits; Jul 17 last US close was risk-off (S&P −1.01%, SOX bear market) while Jul 20 China state-team buying (>500亿 + ~100亿) and CSRC stability talks lifted morning A/H shares.

summary.text: Jul 20 oil (CNBC intraday, not settle): Brent Sep futures rose ~2.54% to top $90/bbl and WTI Aug ~$84.38 after a ninth consecutive night of US strikes (AP) and Jul 19 confirmation of US casualties — two killed in Jordan (Jul 17 attack), unidentified remains recovered, plus one killed in Iraq (Jul 18 drone-ordnance detonation) and one wounded (BBC Jul 19).

Last official US tape Jul 17 (Straits Times): S&P −1.01% to 7,457.69, Nasdaq −1.40% to 25,520.24, Dow −0.77% to 52,146.42; PHLX Semi Index entered a bear market (−20.2% from Jun 22 record). Markets closed Jul 18–19; Jul 20 US session not yet open at publish time.

Jul 19–20 China policy response: 国新 (>500亿元 via buyback-relending program) and 诚通 (~100亿元 recent buys) announced Jul 19 evening (央视网); CSRC convened Jul 20 with listed firms, brokers, and funds (SCMP/21财经); Jul LPR held at 3.0%/3.5% (14th month); PBOC Jul 20 net OMO +1745亿元 at 1.40%.

Jul 20 morning China tape (intraday, not official close): SCMP reports CSI 300 +1.6% and Hang Seng +2.1% in morning trading; 21财经 open had Shanghai +0.73% to 3791.66 after Jul 17 official close −3.05% to 3764.15 (华尔街见闻 3777165). Jul 20 is 长鑫科技 Changxin DRAM IPO payment day (华尔街见闻 3776948).

Jul 20 crypto (CoinDesk/BlockBeats): BTC ~$64,200, roughly flat on the day (CoinDesk Jul 20); week to Jul 19 US spot BTC ETF net inflow 23.86亿美元 (IBIT +25.69亿美元; other ETFs net −1.83亿美元 per BlockBeats/Farside).

signal: Brent intraday >$90 re-arms the oil→Fed channel into Mag 7 earnings week while China’s state-team (>500亿 + ~100亿) and CSRC Jul 20 talks counter Jul 17’s −3.05% Shanghai plunge — split tape between geo inflation and onshore stabilization.
watch: Does Monday Brent hold above Jul 17’s $88.10 settle through the first official post-weekend settle while SOX stays in bear-market territory — or does Jul 20 morning CSI 300 +1.6% mark a durable state-team floor?
globalRegime: Fed holds 3.50%–3.75% with Jul 14–15 CPI/PPI misses still backward-looking while forward energy risk re-accelerated: Brent last settled $88.10 Jul 17 (+15.90% on the week) before Jul 18–19 weekend US-Iran infrastructure strikes and Jul 20 intraday Brent >$90 (CNBC). Jul 17 US equities closed risk-off (S&P −1.01%, Nasdaq −1.40%) as SOX entered a bear market on Kimi K3 / AI-capex doubts. Jul 28–29 FOMC and Mag 7 earnings are the next hard tests.

globalChanged.text: Jul 20 (CNBC intraday): Brent Sep futures rose ~2.54% to top $90/bbl; WTI Aug ~$84.38 (+2.29%) as US completed a ninth consecutive night of strikes on Iranian targets and CENTCOM cited ongoing Hormuz shipping restrictions.

Jul 19 (AP/BBC): two US service members killed and one missing after Jul 17 Jordan attack; unidentified remains recovered Jul 19; separately one service member killed Jul 18 in northern Iraq during controlled detonation of downed Iranian drone ordnance and one wounded.

Jul 18–19 (BBC/AP): Iran hit Kuwait power/desalination and oil facilities for a second day; GCC accused Iran of targeting civilian infrastructure; Iran suspended interim-deal commitments; US reimposed naval blockade on Iranian ports.

Jul 17 last official US close (Straits Times): S&P −1.01% to 7,457.69, Nasdaq −1.40% to 25,520.24, Dow −0.77% to 52,146.42 — weekly losses S&P −1.6%, Nasdaq −2.9%; SOX bear market confirmed (−20.2% from Jun 22 record).
Jul 17 rates/commodities last settle (CNBC): Brent Sep $88.10 (+4.59%, +15.90% on week); WTI Aug $82.49 (+4.48%); 10y Treasury 4.545% (−2bp).
Jul 20 crypto (CoinDesk/BlockBeats): BTC ~$64,200 roughly flat (CoinDesk Jul 20); week to Jul 19 US spot BTC ETF net inflow 23.86亿美元 (BlockBeats/Farside, IBIT +25.69亿美元).

globalImplies.text: Jul 20 Brent >$90 intraday overwrites Jul 14–15 disinflation relief for Jul 28–29 FOMC pricing unless Hormuz crossings normalize quickly.
SOX bear-market threshold plus Brent >$90 sets a hostile backdrop for Alphabet/Tesla/Intel earnings due this week — revenue beats may not lift multiples if oil stays elevated.
CENTCOM casualty count rising (Jordan + Iraq) raises escalation risk into the US reopen even if Jul 17 equity close already priced Kimi K3 semi stress.

globalTensions: Jul 17 SOX bear market vs 23.86亿美元 weekly BTC ETF inflow; Jul 20 Brent >$90 vs BTC ~$64,200 flat; Jul 17 Michigan 54.4 beat vs Brent +15.90% on week and weekend Kuwait hits; state-team China bid vs global semi/AI de-risking.
chinaStance: Moderately loose via quantity (Jul 20 net OMO +1745亿元) and explicit state-team equity support, cautious on price — Jul LPR held 3.0%/3.5% for a 14th month despite Q2 GDP miss and Jul 20 Brent >$90 import-inflation risk; CSRC Jul 20 stability symposium signals administrative backstop after Jul 17 −3.05% Shanghai plunge.

chinaChanged.text: Jul 19 evening (央视网): 国新旗下国新投资已使用股票回购增持专项再贷款及配套资金超500亿元维护市场稳定，将继续增持央企股票；诚通及诚通资本、诚旸投资近日累计买入近百亿元，将继续增持国资央企和科技企业股票及ETF。

Jul 20 (SCMP/21财经): CSRC met listed companies, brokerages, and mutual funds on market stability; morning trading CSI 300 +1.6%, Hang Seng +2.1% (SCMP intraday); Shanghai opened +0.73% to 3791.66 (21财经 09:59).
Jul 20 PBOC (21财经/第一财经): 7-day OMO 3985亿元 at 1.40% with 2240亿元 maturing → net +1745亿元 (1745亿元); 1y LPR 3.0% and 5y+ LPR 3.5% unchanged.

Jul 17 A-share last official close (华尔街见闻 3777165): Shanghai −3.05% to 3764.15 (broke 3800), Shenzhen −5.40%, ChiNext −7.15%, STAR −7%+; turnover ~2.65tn yuan; ~5,000 stocks fell — semis/CPO led losses after Kimi K3.
Jul 20 (华尔街见闻 3776948): 长鑫科技 Changxin DRAM STAR IPO payment day — issue price CNY8.66/share after Jul 16 subscription; proceeds for DRAM capacity and HBM R&D.
Jul 18 (Caixin): SAFE preparing new QDII quota round with larger mutual-fund share after Q2 approved no new quotas — structural outbound-access expansion still in pipeline.
Jul 19 (BlockBeats): US spot Bitcoin ETF weekly net inflow 23.86亿美元 (IBIT +25.69亿美元) — institutional bid persists despite Jul 17 onshore/global risk-off.

chinaImplies.text: State-team announcements (>500亿 + ~100亿) plus CSRC Jul 20 symposium are a direct response to Jul 17 −3.05% Shanghai and STAR bear-market threshold — near-term beta may decouple from SOX if buying persists through the Jul 20 close.
LPR hold and net OMO +1745亿元 confirm quantity easing without price easing — Brent >$90 raises import-inflation constraint on any near-term rate cut.
Changxin payment day (Jul 20) is a concurrent capital drain that could cap rebound follow-through even with state buying — watch turnover vs Jul 17 2.65tn yuan.

chinaDivergences: Jul 20 morning CSI 300 +1.6% vs Jul 17 Shanghai −3.05%; Jul 20 Brent >$90 vs LPR hold; 23.86亿美元 weekly BTC ETF inflow vs Jul 17 onshore −3.05%; state-team equity bid vs global SOX bear market and Kimi K3 narrative.

assetFramework.regime: AI capex-return scrutiny vs oil/geo inflation — Mag 7 earnings as next hard test
assetFramework.driver: Jul 17 S&P −1.01% / Nasdaq −1.40% (last close); SOX bear market; Jul 20 Brent >$90 intraday ahead of US reopen
assetFramework.read: Markets closed Jul 18–19 — Jul 20 Brent >$90 adds gap-down risk to Jul 17 risk-off close unless earnings offset geo/oil
assetFramework.invalidator: US reopen: SOX reclaims 12,400 with Brent first settle <$85

assetFramework.regime: Geo/oil inflation premium vs safe-haven bid — curve bull-steepening
assetFramework.driver: 10y 4.545% Jul 17 (last print); Jul 20 Brent >$90 intraday; Jul 18–19 infrastructure strikes
assetFramework.read: Jul 17 long-end eased on safe-haven flows; Jul 20 oil spike likely pressures belly at US reopen unless strikes de-escalate
assetFramework.invalidator: 10y falls below 4.40% with Brent official settle also below $82 for three sessions

assetFramework.regime: Rate differentials with Hormuz safe-haven bid
assetFramework.driver: Jul 17 risk-off; Brent $88.10 settle then >$90 Jul 20 intraday; Jul 18–19 troop deaths
assetFramework.read: Geo/oil bid likely dominates into US reopen — no fresh DXY close verified Jul 18–20
assetFramework.invalidator: DXY breaks lower while Brent >$90 and SOX stays in bear market after US reopen

assetFramework.driver: Jul 17 spot gold ~$4,016 (+1.00%, 华尔街见闻 3777268); Jul 20 Brent >$90 and weekend infrastructure strikes
assetFramework.read: Last verified spot ~$4,016 Jul 17 — weekend Kuwait/Jordan hits support geo-hedge read into Jul 20
assetFramework.invalidator: Gold falls below $3,950 while Brent also rises at US reopen — hedge floor questioned

assetFramework.regime: Geopolitical supply risk — Hormuz/Kuwait infrastructure premium dominates
assetFramework.driver: Jul 17 Brent settled $88.10; Jul 20 intraday topped $90 (+2.54%, CNBC); ninth night US strikes (CENTCOM/AP)
assetFramework.read: Jul 20 >$90 intraday confirms weekend escalation over Jul 17 $88.10 settle — first official Mon settle is the falsifier checkpoint
assetFramework.invalidator: Brent settles below $82 with Hormuz crossings >40/day for five sessions

assetFramework.regime: State-team stabilization vs global semi-AI beta — event-driven policy pockets
assetFramework.driver: Shanghai −3.05% Jul 17 official close; Jul 20 morning CSI 300 +1.6% (SCMP intraday); state-team >600亿 announced Jul 19
assetFramework.read: Jul 20 morning rebound tests whether administrative backstop offsets Jul 17 Kimi/semi sync — Jul 20 official close pending at publish
assetFramework.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 3,900 on rising volume with SOX stabilizing above 12,000

assetFramework.regime: PBOC fix as policy signal; quantity easing without price easing
assetFramework.driver: Jul 20 net OMO +1745亿元; LPR hold 3.0%/3.5%; Jul 20 USD/CNY fix 6.7948 (−14bp, 21财经); Brent >$90
assetFramework.read: Fix slightly weaker Jul 20 — quantity/structural support active, LPR pivot not signaled; oil spike adds FX management pressure
assetFramework.invalidator: Fix persistently weaker than model estimates for a week

assetFramework.regime: High-beta global liquidity asset in the oil→AI-capex chain
assetFramework.driver: CoinDesk Jul 20 BTC ~$64,200 flat; BlockBeats Jul 19 weekly ETF inflow 23.86亿美元; Jul 17 SOX bear market
assetFramework.read: Split tape — ETF inflows and flat ~$64k vs Jul 20 Brent >$90 inflation channel; neutral until US reopen resolves oil vs liquidity
assetFramework.invalidator: BTC breaks below $62,500 while weekly ETF flows turn negative and SOX falls another −3%

signals.name: Jul 20 Brent intraday >$90 after weekend infrastructure war
signals.evidence: CNBC Jul 20: Brent Sep ~+2.54% topping $90; Jul 17 settle $88.10; Jul 18–19 Kuwait desalination/oil hits and CENTCOM ninth night of strikes (BBC/AP/CENTCOM).
signals.mechanism: Infrastructure strikes widen conflict beyond Hormuz shipping — forward energy inflation overwrites Jul 14–15 CPI/PPI misses ahead of Jul 28–29 FOMC and Mag 7 earnings.
signals.disprovedIf: Monday official Brent settle below $82 with Hormuz crossings >40/day and no new infrastructure strikes for five sessions.

signals.name: China state-team (>600亿 announced) + CSRC Jul 20 stability push
signals.evidence: Jul 19 央视网: 国新 >500亿元 program use + 诚通 ~100亿元 recent buys; Jul 20 CSRC symposium (SCMP); morning CSI 300 +1.6% after Jul 17 Shanghai −3.05% (华尔街见闻 3777165).
signals.mechanism: Explicit administrative backstop after Kimi/semi-driven Jul 17 plunge — state buying can decouple onshore beta from SOX near term if sustained through Jul 20 close.
signals.disprovedIf: Shanghai closes below 3,700 on Jul 20 despite announced buying and CSRC meeting.

signals.name: SOX bear market persists into Jul 20 oil spike
signals.evidence: Jul 17 SOX −20.2% from Jun 22 record (Straits Times); Nasdaq −1.40% same session; Kimi K3 Jul 17 triggered global semi de-risking (华尔街见闻 3777165).
signals.mechanism: Open-weight Chinese frontier model challenges scarce-AI pricing — bear-market threshold makes AI capex the falsifier into earnings week; Jul 20 oil adds dual headwind.
signals.disprovedIf: SOX reclaims 12,400 within five sessions of US reopen with no further Kimi-weighted headlines.

signals.name: BTC ~$64,200 flat vs Brent >$90 and ETF inflow 23.86亿美元
signals.evidence: CoinDesk Jul 20 BTC ~$64,200 flat; BlockBeats Jul 19 weekly ETF inflow 23.86亿美元 (IBIT +25.69亿美元).
signals.mechanism: Institutional spot bid holds while oil inflation channel re-engages — either front-running Fed hold or crowded relief trade into Jul 29.
signals.disprovedIf: Weekly ETF flows turn negative and BTC closes below $62,500 for three sessions post-US reopen.

signals.name: LPR hold (14th month) vs Brent >$90 import-inflation risk
signals.evidence: Jul 20 LPR 3.0%/3.5% unchanged (第一财经); Jul 20 Brent >$90 intraday (CNBC); Jul 20 net OMO +1745亿元 (21财经).
signals.mechanism: Price tool frozen while quantity/structural easing continues — oil spike narrows room for LPR cuts even as state team buys equities.
signals.disprovedIf: August LPR cut with Brent official settle below $85 for two weeks.

signals.name: Changxin IPO payment-day capital drain (Jul 20)
signals.evidence: 华尔街见闻 3776948: Changxin DRAM IPO payment Jul 20 after Jul 16 subscription at CNY8.66/share; Jul 17 Shanghai −3.05% on semi/Kimi stress.
signals.mechanism: Large STAR IPO payment can absorb liquidity concurrent with state-team buying — may cap rebound follow-through.
signals.disprovedIf: Jul 20 turnover exceeds Jul 17 2.65tn yuan with Shanghai closing green despite payment day.

watchItems.headline: Jul 20 Brent intraday >$90 vs Jul 17 $88.10 settle
watchItems.why: STRONG weekend Gulf/oil escalation signal — infrastructure strikes and US casualties.
watchItems.watch: First official Mon Brent settle; Hormuz crossing counts; CENTCOM strike headlines.
watchItems.trigger: Official Brent settle ≥$90 and holds through Tue.
watchItems.invalidator: Official Brent settle below $82 with crossings >40/day for five days.

watchItems.headline: China state-team bid + Jul 20 CSRC symposium
watchItems.why: STRONG onshore stabilization signal after Jul 17 −3.05% Shanghai.
watchItems.watch: Jul 20 official Shanghai close vs 3764.15; CSI 300 morning +1.6% (SCMP); 国新/诚通 follow-through.
watchItems.trigger: Shanghai closes above 3,850 on turnover >2.5tn yuan with STAR stabilizing.
watchItems.invalidator: Shanghai closes below 3,700 despite Jul 19 announcements.

watchItems.headline: SOX bear-market depth into Mag 7 earnings
watchItems.why: STRONG Kimi K3 / AI-capex signal — Jul 20 oil adds gap risk.
watchItems.watch: SOX vs Jul 17 bear-market threshold; US reopen vs 11,500; Alphabet/Tesla/Intel dates this week.
watchItems.trigger: SOX closes below 11,500 on US reopen with S&P also −1%.
watchItems.invalidator: SOX reclaims 12,400 and Nasdaq positive two straight sessions.

watchItems.headline: CENTCOM casualties and interim-deal collapse
watchItems.why: STRONG weekend escalation — Jordan + Iraq deaths and Kuwait infrastructure hits.
watchItems.watch: CENTCOM statements; Iran interim-deal status; GCC infrastructure strike headlines.
watchItems.trigger: Third consecutive day of Gulf desalination/power strikes with Brent official settle ≥$90.
watchItems.invalidator: Mediation restart and Hormuz crossings exceed 40/day for three sessions.

watchItems.headline: BTC ~$64,200 flat vs 23.86亿美元 ETF inflow
watchItems.why: MODERATE crypto divergence from Jul 17 risk-off and Jul 20 oil spike.
watchItems.watch: Weekly ETF flow update; BTC vs CoinDesk ~$64,200 cite; US reopen reaction.
watchItems.trigger: BTC holds $64k through US open despite Brent official settle ≥$90.
watchItems.invalidator: Weekly ETF flows negative and BTC below $62,500 for three sessions.

sources: CNBC (Jul 20 Brent/WTI intraday; Jul 17 settle/Treasury), AP (Jul 19 casualties), AP (Jul 19 airstrikes), BBC (Jul 18–19 US/Iran), Straits Times (Jul 17 US close; SOX bear market), CoinDesk (Jul 20 BTC), BlockBeats/律动 (303598 Jul 19 ETF flows), 央视网 (Jul 19 state-team buying), SCMP (Jul 20 A-share rebound), 第一财经 (Jul 20 LPR), 21财经 (Jul 20 open/OMO/fix), 华尔街见闻 (3777165 Jul 17 close; 3776948 Changxin payment; 3777268 Jul 17 spot gold), Caixin Global (Jul 18 QDII).
singleSource: Rejected BlockBeats 303605/303615 (BTC $120k/$118k flashes — wrong-year/stale vs Jul 2026 ~$64k tape). Rejected 华尔街见闻 3751205/3751275 (2025 stale wraps). Jul 20 China index moves labeled intraday/morning where official Jul 20 close unavailable at publish. SCMP '60 billion yuan' headline omitted — 央视网 primary cites >500亿元 (国新 program) and ~100亿元 (诚通 recent) separately. No Jul 20 华尔街见闻 breakfast published at generate time — used 3777165 (Jul 17 close) and 3776948 (Jul 20 Changxin payment) within 72h window. Glassnode Insights inbox (2026-W30) was a subscription welcome only — no Week on Chain on-chain data to merge.

marketDashboard.note: Equities, FX, gold futures, and Brent use the latest two Yahoo daily closes; US Treasuries use the official daily yield curve (bp vs prior print); BTC is CoinGecko 24h change; funding compares adjacent OKX prints. Snapshot captured at generate time — not live on the page.
